name: Finance
description: Double-entry accounting v1: owner-scoped CRUD on a chart of accounts (fin_accounts), journal headers (fin_journals) and their debit/credit lines (fin_journal_lines), and recurring-journal templates (fin_recurring), plus one report (trial balance, computed by object_finance.trial_balance() over posted journals only). Reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo (its finance/ app: 'Currency, Entity ..., Account (COA hierarchy, 5 types), Journal (+lines; draft/posted; ...), JournalLine (debit-or-credit, ...), ... RecurringJournal (JSON template + frequency + auto_post)'). Journal totals (total_debits_cents/total_credits_cents/is_balanced) are COMPUTED PROPERTIES via object_finance.journal_totals(), never stamped onto a record and never enforced -- posting a journal (draft->posted, owner-gated) is a bare status flip with no balance check, matching the source's own documented weak spot exactly (reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo: 'posting is a bare status flip -- balance NOT enforced at post'). There is no totals-stamping HANDLES handler in this package, unlike app-invoices' invoice_totals or app-orders' order_totals -- that absence is deliberate, not an oversight; see object_finance.py's module docstring. Deferred to a later slice, per the reconciled predecessor finance model (private source audit, not part of this repo): balance ENFORCEMENT at post (a transition guard checking is_balanced -- a real improvement over the source, tracked here, not built in this migration); auto-posting journal entries from other collections (stock moves, invoices, orders, depreciation) -- no such collection currently writes a fin_journals/fin_journal_lines row, matching the source's own gap ('journal FKs from stock/invoice/depreciation stay null -- no auto-posting integration'); the recurring-journal daemon materialization job (fin_recurring's fields are carried, the scheduled pass that reads them and creates real journals is not); profit & loss, balance sheet, and cash flow reports (same fold shape as trial_balance, addable later -- trial_balance is the only report v1 ships); statement-line bank-import reconciliation; closed/locked accounting periods; multi-currency FX (the source's JournalLine carried exchange_rate/base_amount for a line's currency to differ from its journal's; this package's lines carry only debit_cents/credit_cents in the journal's own currency -- no exchange_rate field exists here, so this is a dropped field, not a carried-but-unused one, and multi-currency journals are out of scope for v1). Chart-of-accounts entity-mode seeding (the source's Entity model auto-seeds a starter chart of accounts per books-mode) is also not built -- fin_accounts rows are created by hand like every other collection's header-only seed. Account and trial-balance detail permalinks (/accounts/{id}, /trial-balance) still need site_routes records -- not seeded by this package, same precedent as app-invoices' /invoices/{invoice_id:uuid} and app-catalog's /products/{product_id:uuid}. The journal permalink /journals/{journal_id:uuid} is now a seeded 59 detail view (site_view_render): a composed page of three generic block renderers -- an editable+deletable `detail` block (fin_journals; the draft->posted transition is now an edit to the status field in the owner's edit form, not a one-click Post button -- same make-it-basic trade as app-forum's pin/lock, guarded the same way by schemas/fin_journals.json's status transitions), a `related` block (fin_journal_lines by journal_id, RELATIONAL -- an append-only event-log of ledger postings, not embedded, per the attachability criterion), and a `form` block with fixed:{journal_id:$record_id} (Add Posting, FK locked to the page's journal). Debit/credit *_cents render through /form's built-in cents formatting on the related list, same as every other *_cents field in this codebase -- no custom code. Dropped, not carried forward: the bespoke page's live-computed total-debits/total-credits/is-balanced summary strip (object_finance.journal_totals(), folded client-side over the fetched lines) has no generic block equivalent and is not rebuilt here; a balance summary would need its own report-style block (same category as trial_balance), tracked as future work, not this migration. entity_id (65 multi-entity, already on fin_journals/fin_journal_lines) needs no special handling in the composed view -- it renders as an ordinary relation field in the generic detail/edit form. object_finance.py (journal_totals/trial_balance) lives at the repository root alongside object_records.py -- a plain importable library, not a registered object in this manifest, the same way object_records.py itself is never a package object.
